headline: AI security techniques and retrieval infrastructure broke out together this week, led by vulnerability detection and vector search.
body: A cluster of defensive techniques surged in parallel: vulnerability detection, guardrails, prompt-injection defense, and risk assessment all moved as leaders, alongside an argument that generative AI has lowered the barrier to cyberattacks and demands an assume-breach posture. Retrieval infrastructure broke out in tandem, with semantic chunking, vector search, and HNSW entering as movers, and kernel fusion appearing as a new outlier. On the agent side, research cited in a documentary trailer found 80% of organizations deploy AI agents while only 10% feel in control, sharpening the focus on human oversight. Separate Compile 26 talks pushed on efficiency and memory, including a key-value cache compaction method for long-horizon agents.

cited_items.title: Why We're Not Too Late to Stop AI Hackers | Nebulock, Damien Lewke
AI Agents: How Do We Take Back Control? | Trailer
